div.h1: Algorithm Steps: Mask Shifting for Alignment

p.strong: Simulating mask >>= offset to adjust bitmask for unaligned string start
p.em: Instruction Context: Intel® AVX-512 / SSE VPCMPB / PMOVMSKB Emulation

g.text: 3. AFTER: Normalized for Leading Zero Count

ul.li: The vector load contains garbage from [ptr-8, ptr-1].
To ignore this, we KSHIFTRW right by the byte offset.
Bit 11 corresponds to the 4th valid byte (8+3).
Trailing Zero Count (tzcnt) on 0x0008 correctly yields Index 3.
This index is relative to the logical start of the string.

g.text: Align Valid String Data to Bit 0
